summary: AnyMCP submission requires a GitHub Pull Request adding a .NET C# (.cs) MCP server file to the mcp/ directory of github.com/xakpc/anymcp-io. This is not automatable from this environment due to: (1) technology mismatch — my-automaton is a Node.js MCP server, AnyMCP exclusively hosts C#/.NET servers; (2) no GITHUB_TOKEN available for automated PR creation; (3) MCP endpoint (https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp) returns 404. A new .NET 10 C# MCP server would need to be developed to proxy or reimplement the my-automaton tools.

submission_process.file_format: .cs file with YAML front matter in C# comments

blockers.detail: AnyMCP exclusively hosts .NET C# MCP servers. my-automaton is implemented in Node.js (JavaScript). No C#/.NET codebase exists.
AnyMCP requires a single .cs file in the mcp/ directory with YAML front matter. No such file exists.
GITHUB_TOKEN is not available in the environment. Automated PR creation is not possible.
The my-automaton MCP endpoint (https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp) returns HTTP 404.

generated_artifacts.description: Complete research on AnyMCP platform, submission process, and requirements
Previous AnyMCP submission result with same conclusions
This file — final result for AnyMCP directory submission task

recommendation: If submission to AnyMCP is desired, develop a new .NET 10 C# MCP server that either (a) acts as a proxy to my-automaton's HTTP API at https://automation.songheng.vip, or (b) reimplements the my-automaton MCP tools in C#. Then submit via GitHub PR to xakpc/anymcp-io following the CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines.
manual_instructions_summary: To submit manually: (1) Fork https://github.com/xakpc/anymcp-io (2) Create a .cs file in mcp/ with YAML front matter (id, name, description, author) (3) Add C# MCP server code using ModelContextProtocol package (4) Submit a Pull Request with description of server name, features, and requirements
